description_long: This object is a stylized, futuristic airplane with a distinctive X-shaped wing configuration, reminiscent of science fiction spacecraft. The fuselage is elongated and tapers to a pointed nose, with two cylindrical engines mounted on each side, for a total of four engines. The wings are straight and splayed outward, each ending in a long, narrow extension that resembles a laser cannon or antenna. The tail section is minimal, with no visible vertical stabilizer, and the landing gear is not visible, suggesting a streamlined, possibly retractable design. The surface appears smooth and untextured, with a uniform light gray color throughout.
description: A futuristic, X-wing style airplane with four cylindrical engines, splayed wings, and a smooth, light gray surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed nose, four cylindrical engines, and the X-shaped wing configuration.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, engine placement, and the long, narrow wing extensions.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the symmetry of the wings and engines, with the tail section visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring view 1, showing the same engine and wing structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight, splayed wings forming an X-shape; no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles; each wing features a long, narrow extension at the tip.
attribute_engine_type: Four cylindrical jet engines mounted in pairs on each side of the fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Minimal tail section with no visible vertical stabilizer; lacks conventional, T-tail, or V-tail features.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; configuration indeterminate from images.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit integrated into the nose of the fuselage; appears to be single-seat with a streamlined canopy.
